title: rubus pubescens subsp. eupubescens
intro: Rubus pubescens subsp. eupubescens, also known as the dwarf raspberry or creeping raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus pubescens plant, a type of wild raspberry native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health issues such as arthritis, digestive problems, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Rubus pubescens subsp. eupubescens include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions and promote overall well-being.
